effect_package_explanations.asthought: cast/play permissions such as flash or alternate-zone casting
effect_package_explanations.combat: attack/block restrictions, evasion, combat taxes, and combat permissions
effect_package_explanations.continuous: continuous rules, type/color/control changes, replacement-like rule modifiers
effect_package_explanations.cost: cost increases, reductions, alternate or special cost handling
effect_package_explanations.counter: counter placement, removal, proliferation, and counter conditions
effect_package_explanations.enterAttribute: as-enters choices and permanent entry attributes
effect_package_explanations.turn: extra turns, skip steps, end turn, and turn-modifying effects

ability_timing.manaloom_use: Use ability classes to classify runtime executor families and focused test setup.

ai_heuristics.manaloom_use: Reference after rules correctness improves; avoid blocking card-rule work on AI parity.
ai_heuristics.why: XMage AI can inform targeting/play-priority heuristics but is less directly portable than rules/tests.

card_implementations.manaloom_use: Resolve card -> XMage class, extract effect/cost/target signals, then gate with ManaLoom tests and PG precheck.
card_implementations.why: Per-card implementation classes are the fastest exact reference for card behavior.

commander_legality.manaloom_use: Use as a cross-check for partner/background and command-zone metadata, not as PostgreSQL source of truth.
commander_legality.why: XMage has Commander, partner, background, companion-style validator references and command-zone handling.

costs_and_cost_adjusters.manaloom_use: Map XMage cost adjusters/effects to explicit ManaLoom cost contracts instead of one-off card patches.
costs_and_cost_adjusters.why: Cost reducers, alternate costs, additional costs, X costs, and payment restrictions are common high-severity blockers.

dynamic_values_conditions.manaloom_use: Attach conditional fields to effect_json and generate edge-case assertions.
dynamic_values_conditions.why: Dynamic values and conditions explain variable damage, counts, controller choices, thresholds, and conditional modes.

effect_library.manaloom_use: Build an effect-class taxonomy and map frequent classes to ManaLoom effect_json templates.
effect_library.why: XMage's effect classes are reusable vocabulary for destroy, exile, copy, token, draw, prevention, replacement, and continuous effects.

game_events_state.manaloom_use: Compare ManaLoom event contracts to XMage event taxonomy and close static event-contract gaps in batches.
game_events_state.why: Game events and state application define what a replay/audit should be able to observe.

priority_stack_turn_engine.manaloom_use: Use as conformance spec for stack/priority tests; do not port the whole engine blindly.
priority_stack_turn_engine.why: Priority, phase/step order, stack resolution, passed-priority flags, and SBA loops define correctness of non-goldfish tests.

targets_filters_predicates.manaloom_use: Generate target constraints, legality checks, and focused target revalidation tests.
targets_filters_predicates.why: Target and filter classes encode legality and scope, which are frequent sources of false confidence.

test_scenario_corpus.manaloom_use: Mine matching card tests and convert their scenario shape into focused ManaLoom replay/unit tests.
test_scenario_corpus.why: XMage tests contain a mature scenario grammar for addCard/castSpell/activateAbility/setChoice/waitStackResolved/check* assertions.

watchers_replacement_prevention.manaloom_use: Use as an event-contract and state-memory reference, especially for first spell, damage, draw, discard, replacement, and prevention.
watchers_replacement_prevention.why: Watchers, replacement, prevention, and continuous rule-modifying effects are where many battle-runtime lineage gaps originate.

recommendations.next_action: Keep XMage as reference corpus and mine stable contracts into ManaLoom runtime/tests.
recommendations.reason: ManaLoom battle is Python/Hermes/PostgreSQL driven; wholesale Java engine integration would be slower than absorbing contracts and test cases.

recommendations.item: Promote effect-class taxonomy before more card-by-card work.
recommendations.next_action: Extend xmage_to_manaloom_effect_hints.py using the inventory's effect packages and suffix counts.
recommendations.reason: Most remaining high/medium cards are instances of reusable effect/cost/target families.

recommendations.item: Mine XMage tests into focused ManaLoom scenarios.
recommendations.next_action: Add a local test-miner that finds card-name tests and emits ManaLoom scenario candidates.
recommendations.reason: XMage tests already encode setup/action/assertion flow for stack, choices, costs, replacement, and timing.

recommendations.item: Use priority/stack/turn classes as conformance reference.
recommendations.next_action: Create ManaLoom stack/priority contract tests for pass priority, response windows, stack copies, and SBA loops.
recommendations.reason: Current battle gates are sensitive to cast permission, stack timing, and event-contract gaps.

recommendations.item: Use GameEvent/Watcher taxonomy for event-contract coverage.
recommendations.next_action: Compare ManaLoom event_contract_static taxonomy against XMage GameEvent.EventType and watcher families.
recommendations.reason: Latest battle review_required state still points at static event-contract style gaps, not deck-list incoherence.

recommendations.item: Use target/filter/predicate classes to harden effect_json target constraints.
recommendations.next_action: Map common Target*/Filter*/Predicate* classes to explicit target_scope and valid_zone fields.
recommendations.reason: Target legality mistakes produce false positives in battle and poor AI choices.

recommendations.item: Use Commander validators as cross-check only.
recommendations.next_action: Keep a read-only commander legality audit and do not let XMage overwrite PG metadata.
recommendations.reason: PostgreSQL remains source of truth, but XMage validator coverage is useful to catch partner/background drift.

recommendations.next_action: Reference AI packages only after runtime rule gates stabilize.
recommendations.reason: Rule correctness and test scaling have better return right now than porting AI decision code.
